description_long: This object is a long, slender sword with a distinctive wavy blade, reminiscent of a flamberge or flame-bladed sword. The blade is double-edged and features a pronounced undulating pattern along its entire length, giving it a visually striking and aggressive appearance. The crossguard is simple and slightly curved, providing basic hand protection, while the handle is straight and appears to be wrapped or textured for grip. The pommel is minimalistic, blending seamlessly with the handle. The entire sword has a dark metallic finish, suggesting it is forged from steel or a similar metal, with no visible ornamentation or embellishments. Its overall design is utilitarian yet elegant, suitable for ceremonial or combat use.
description: A long, wavy-bladed sword with a dark metallic finish, simple crossguard, and straight handle. The blade's undulating pattern is its most distinctive feature.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wavy blade, crossguard, and straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and the minimal thickness of the crossguard and handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again highlighting the undulating blade and simple handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the blade's thinness and straight alignment.
attribute_handle_design: straight, cylindrical, dark, possibly wrapped or textured for grip
